content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 16,
1994
Gordon Phillips
Deputy City Attorney
City of Alameda
2263 Santa Clara Avenue
Alameda,
CA 94501-4456
Re:
Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No.
1—94—377
Dear Mr. Phillips:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(“the Act”) •1/
Because your question is
general in nature,
we consider your letter to be a request for
informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c) (3) 2/
Your letter, dated November 29,
1994, accurately summarizes
my advice that the members of the Economic Development Commission
must file an initial Statement of Economic Interests,
Form 730,
within 30 days of the effective date of the conflict of interest
code.
As we discussed, the members are not required to file
statements prior to inclusion of the Commission in the City of
Alameda’s conflict of interest code.
The members will be required to disclose reportable
investments, business positions,
and interests in real property
held on the effective date of the conflict of interest code and
income received during the 12 months before the effective date of
the conflict of interest code.
(Section 87302.)
1/
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000,
q.
All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2/
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Gordon Phillips
File No.
1—94—377
Page Two
If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact
me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Dixie Howard
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division

sections.conclusion: Your letter, dated November 29, 1994, accurately summarizes my advice that the members of the Economic Development Commission must file an initial Statement of Economic Interests, Form 730, within 30 days of the effective date of the conflict of interest code. As we discussed, the members are not required to file statements prior to inclusion of the Commission in the City of Alameda's conflict of interest code. The members will be required to disclose reportable investments, business positions, and interests in real property held on the effective date of the conflict of interest code and income received during the 12 months before the effective date of the conflict of interest code. (Section 87302.)
sections.question_synthetic: Must members of an Economic Development Commission file initial Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) before or after their commission is included in the city's conflict of interest code, and what disclosures are required?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Economic Development Commission members must file Form 730 within 30 days of the conflict of interest code's effective date, but are not required to file before the commission is included in the code. They must disclose investments, business positions, real property interests as of the effective date, and income from the 12 months preceding it.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Letter confirms prior telephone advice; characterized as informal assistance under Regulation 18329(c)(3), which does not provide immunity. No formal Q/C section headers, but question and conclusion clearly present in body text.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Must members of an Economic Development Commission file initial Statements of Economic Interests (Form 730) before or after their commission is included in the city's conflict of interest code, and what disclosures are required?

CONCLUSION: Your letter, dated November 29, 1994, accurately summarizes my advice that the members of the Economic Development Commission must file an initial Statement of Economic Interests, Form 730, within 30 days of the effective date of the conflict of interest code. As we discussed, the members are not required to file statements prior to inclusion of the Commission in the City of Alameda's conflict of interest code. The members will be required to disclose reportable investments, business positions, and interests in real property held on the effective date of the conflict of interest code and income received during the 12 months before the effective date of the conflict of interest code. (Section 87302.)

As we discussed, the members are not required to file statements prior to inclusion of the Commission in the City of Alameda’s conflict of interest code. The members will be required to disclose reportable investments, business positions, and interests in real property held on the effective date of the conflict of interest code and income received during the 12 months before the effective date of the conflict of interest code. (Section 87302.) 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, q. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).) 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660 Gordon Phillips File No. 1—94—377 Page Two If you have questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Dixie Howard Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division
embedding.summary: This is an FPPC confirmation letter to the Deputy City Attorney of Alameda regarding conflict of interest disclosure requirements for Economic Development Commission members under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC confirms prior telephone advice that commission members must file initial Statements of Economic Interests within 30 days of their inclusion in the conflict of interest code.
